sentence: Subjective:
The patient, a 54-year-old male, presents with a 2-3 month history of stabbing, shooting pain in the right cheek bone area of his face, which can last from a few minutes to up to an hour.
He describes the pain as excruciating and states that it is triggered by certain temperatures, cold weather, and wind.
He has tried ibuprofen, Motrin, and tiger balm without relief.
He denies any sensory loss, weakness, or numbness in the affected area.
He has occasional headaches but no history of migraines.
He rates the pain as a 10 out of 10 on a pain scale.
Past medical history:
The patient has no history of multiple sclerosis, brain tumors, or other neurological conditions.
Review of systems:
The patient denies any symptoms of numbness, tingling, or weakness in the affected area.
He has occasional headaches but no history of migraines.
Current medications:
I prescribed Tylenol as needed for headaches.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam:
The patient's face appears symmetric, and he is able to follow my finger in both directions.
He shows no weakness on the right side of his face when smiling.
He is able to hear a sound on both sides of his face when I rub my fingers next to his ear.
When I run cotton tape along the side of his face, he reports a little bit of numbness on the right side.
When I poke him with a needle, he reports a dull sensation on the right side and a sharp sensation on the left side.
Test Results:
MRI results show a small blood vessel abutting and possibly pinching the trigeminal nerve.
Assessment / Problem List:
The patient has trigeminal neuralgia, a condition where compression of the trigeminal nerve causes shooting, electrical pain in the face.
Problem list: Trigeminal neuralgia; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Tegretol  [DOSE] [FREQUENCY] for the patient's trigeminal neuralgia.
We will follow up in one month to assess the effectiveness of the medication and discuss surgical options if necessary.
I will send a prescription to the patient's pharmacy for Tegretol.
I will also perform genetic testing to ensure the patient is a good candidate for the medication.
Follow-up:
The patient will follow up with me in one month to assess the effectiveness of the medication and discuss further treatment options.
